name: 749 Old Marion Road Gym
description: 1 public indoor court you can reserve in advance at low cost, no membership required. They have a new net you can use. You will have the place to yourself. Look them up on Facebook “749 Old Marion Road Gym”
open_play_schedule: Can reserve between 8 and 3 weekdays and in weekends

name: AirFX Trampoline Park
description: The gym is reserved for indoor soccer through the end of February. Pickleball courts are not available at this time.

name: Backyard BBQ
description: Backyard BBQ is a place to play pickleball in Cedar Rapids, IA. There are 2 indoor concrete and hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts can be reserved. A one-time fee is required to play. Players have access to food, restrooms, water, lights, and wheelchair accessibility.

name: Bever Park Tennis Courts
description: Come play pickleball at Bever Park Tennis Courts in Cedar Rapids, IA! There is 1 outdoor hard court. You’ll have to create your own lines and bring your own net. The court is free.

name: Cedar River Landing
description: Doesn't have pickleball court.

name: Collins Aerospace Recreation Center
description: Collins Aerospace Recreation Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Cedar Rapids, IA. There are 9 hard and wood courts. 6 are indoors and 3 are outdoors. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Amenities include restrooms, water, and lights. A membership is required to play.

name: Ellis Park Tennis Courts
description: Ellis Park Tennis Courts is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Cedar Rapids, IA. There are 2 outdoor hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are free.

name: Pickle Palace
description: Pickle Palace is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Cedar Rapids, IA. There are 6 asphalt courts. 2 are outdoors and 4 are indoors. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to food, restrooms, water, lights, a pro shop or equipment store, trainers or lessons, and wheelchair accessibility. A one-time fee is required to play. Reservations are available.
fees: Fee required — Free membership, $30/hr M-Th, $40/hr Fri-Sat-Sun

name: Smithfield Tennis & Pickleball Center
description: Smithfield Tennis & Pickleball Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Cedar Rapids, IA. There are 14 hard and asphalt courts. 8 are indoors and 6 are outdoors. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to restrooms, food, a pro shop or equipment store, locker rooms, water, trainers or lessons, lights, wheelchair accessibility, and youth programming. A membership is required to play. You can reserve the courts.
